title: Crispy Japanese Tangerine Peel Stir-fry
description: A tangy and crispy Japanese dish featuring freshly chopped parsley, simmered for a quick and easy crowd-pleaser that's sugar-free-friendly.
instructions: Wash and pat dry the tangerine peel.
Slice off any tough parts of the peel, then finely chop the remaining peel.
In a large skillet over medium heat, add the chopped tangerine peel and minced garlic. Sauté until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add red pepper flakes and soy sauce to the skillet; stir well for another minute.
Gradually pour in water or chicken broth while continuously stirring to prevent burning. Let it simmer for about 5-7 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in chopped parsley just before serving to keep its vibrant color and texture.
